name: Southern Corn Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrape corn from cooked ears, which yields about 2 cups.
Alternatively, use a can of creamed style corn if fresh corn on the cob isn't available.
2. Separate the yolks from the whites of the eggs and beat each separately.
3. Heat the milk and add it to the beaten egg yolks.
4. Add sugar and flour; mix well.
5. Add corn, butter, salt, and pepper to the milk and egg mixture.
6. Stir in the well-beaten egg whites.
7. Pour the mixture into a greased casserole dish.
8. Sprinkle with crushed corn flakes.
9. Bake for 45 minutes at 350 degrees.
